metadata.description: Comprehensive scoring rubric for evaluating Europass-compliant CVs
metadata.purpose: Grade CV quality, identify gaps, provide benchmark feedback, track improvement
metadata.introduced_due_to: Need for objective CV quality assessment and continuous improvement metrics

content_quality.description: Quality and impact of achievements and experience descriptions

criteria.description: All achievements include measurable metrics and business impact

scoring_rules.90_100: All work experience achievements include specific metrics (numbers, percentages, time saved, cost reduction, users impacted, etc.); business impact connected to outcomes
scoring_rules.75_89: 80%+ of achievements quantified; most have business impact context; minor gaps in senior-level positions
scoring_rules.60_74: 50-79% of achievements quantified; some business impact; many responsibilities lack metrics
scoring_rules.40_59: 25-49% of achievements quantified; minimal business impact; mostly responsibility-focused
scoring_rules.0_39: Fewer than 25% quantified; no business impact context; purely responsibility-based

criteria.description: For roles with 6+ years experience, clear demonstration of increasing responsibility and leadership

scoring_rules.90_100: Clear career progression narrative; strategic decision-making evident; mentorship/team leadership demonstrated; scope of work increases with tenure; strategic impact clear
scoring_rules.75_89: Good progression narrative; some strategic decisions shown; limited mentorship mention; generally increasing scope
scoring_rules.60_74: Progression visible but not prominent; minimal strategic positioning; lacks seniority signals in achievement framing
scoring_rules.40_59: Progression unclear; mostly IC (individual contributor) framing; no strategic positioning
scoring_rules.0_39: No career progression narrative; all positions presented as equivalent; no seniority indicators

criteria.description: Achievement statements include scale context and business relevance (not just technical details)

scoring_rules.90_100: Achievements frame impact in business terms (revenue, cost, customers, compliance, efficiency); scale context included (system size, user base, transaction volume)
scoring_rules.75_89: Most achievements have business context; scale mentioned in 70%+ of roles; some purely technical language
scoring_rules.60_74: Mixed business vs. technical framing; scale mentioned in <70%; mostly technical perspective
scoring_rules.40_59: Primarily technical perspective; minimal business context; scale rarely mentioned
scoring_rules.0_39: Pure technical description; no business context; no scale context

criteria.description: Every claim is supported by evidence; no unsubstantiated assertions

scoring_rules.90_100: All skills have supporting evidence (projects, certifications, years of use); all achievements traceable to role context
scoring_rules.75_89: 90%+ of skills have evidence; most claims supported; minimal gaps
scoring_rules.60_74: 70-89% of skills evidenced; some claims lack support
scoring_rules.40_59: 50-69% of skills evidenced; many unsubstantiated claims
scoring_rules.0_39: Fewer than 50% evidenced; many unsupported assertions

structure_compliance.description: Adherence to Europass format and structural requirements

criteria.description: Work experience, education, certifications ordered newest to oldest

scoring_rules.75: Work experience ordered; education/certs have 1-2 minor inconsistencies
scoring_rules.50: Major work experience role out of order; dates inconsistent

criteria.description: All required sections present with appropriate content

scoring_rules.100: All 10 sections present and complete: Personal Info, Profile, Work, Education, Languages, Skills, Certifications, Publications (if relevant), Additional Info
scoring_rules.0: Fewer than 7 sections or critical missing information

criteria.description: Dates in DD/MM/YYYY format; no first-person pronouns; consistent layout

scoring_rules.100: All dates in DD/MM/YYYY or 'Month YYYY'; zero first-person pronouns; consistent formatting throughout
scoring_rules.80: 90%+ dates correct format; 1-2 sentences with first-person; minor layout inconsistencies
scoring_rules.60: 70-89% dates correct; 3-5 first-person instances; several layout issues
scoring_rules.40: 50-69% dates correct; multiple first-person uses; poor layout
scoring_rules.0: Dates in wrong format; first-person throughout; inconsistent layout

tone_language.description: Professional, objective tone; no buzzwords or marketing language

criteria.description: No Americanized buzzwords or unsubstantiated self-praise

scoring_rules.100: Zero buzzwords (rockstar, ninja, guru, passionate, dynamic, go-getter, results-driven, etc.)
scoring_rules.90: 1 minor buzzword detected and appropriately contextualized
scoring_rules.70: 2-3 buzzwords or weak language; partially addressed
scoring_rules.0: 6+ buzzwords; heavy marketing language; unprofessional tone

criteria.description: Facts-based claims; no unsubstantiated personality traits or subjective assessments

scoring_rules.100: 100% objective; all claims fact-based; zero subjective personality descriptors
scoring_rules.70: 90-94% objective; some subjective language present but minimal
scoring_rules.50: 80-89% objective; multiple subjective claims; mixed tone
scoring_rules.0: Majority subjective; heavy personality-focused language; opinion-based

evidence_validation.description: All claims validated; proficiency aligned with experience; no hallucination

criteria.description: Claimed skill proficiency matches years of experience and role evidence

scoring_rules.100: All skills proficiency levels justified by experience duration and role context
scoring_rules.80: 90%+ of skills appropriately proficiency-leveled; 1-2 minor inconsistencies

criteria.description: Certifications are current, recognized, and properly dated

scoring_rules.100: All certifications from recognized bodies; current or recently expired only; dates accurate; expiration tracked
scoring_rules.85: 95%+ valid; 1 minor issue (old cert without note, or date format inconsistency)
scoring_rules.70: Mostly valid; 1-2 non-standard certifications; some date issues
scoring_rules.0: Multiple unrecognized certifications; outdated credentials; missing dates

completeness.description: No missing critical information; comprehensive coverage without fluff

criteria.description: All relevant experience, education, and skills captured without redundancy

scoring_rules.100: All career history >6 months included; all degrees/certs listed; comprehensive skill inventory; no gaps >3 months unexplained
scoring_rules.85: 95%+ of career history; may consolidate roles >10 years; all major certs; 1 minor gap
scoring_rules.70: 90%+ coverage; some old experience consolidated; minor omissions
scoring_rules.50: 80-89% coverage; some experience missing; gaps not explained
scoring_rules.0: <80% coverage; significant career history missing; unexplained gaps

scale.recommendation: No changes needed; CV is highly competitive
Ready to submit; consider minor refinements for maximum impact
Address flagged sections before submission; improvements recommended
Major revisions needed in achievements quantification and business impact
Significant restructuring needed; recommend professional review

key_improvements: Added business impact context (system scale, transaction volume, customer count)
Positioned mainframe expertise as strategic, specialization-based achievement
Restructured experience to show career progression (2013-2026 trajectory)
Converted responsibilities to achievements with quantified metrics
Reorganized skills by expertise tier (core, supporting, emerging)

key_gaps: Achievements lack business impact context (-15 points)
Mainframe expertise not positioned for senior level (-10 points)
Skills not organized by expertise tier (-7 points)

continuous_improvement_tracking.purpose: Track CV quality improvements over time and iterations
metrics_to_track: Average dimension scores (content, structure, tone, evidence, completeness)
Most common gaps (buzzbwords, quantification, business impact)
Score distribution (how many CVs in each rating band)
Candidate feedback (did they advance after using improved CV?)
continuous_improvement_tracking.reporting_cadence: Weekly aggregate; per-candidate on each submission

integration_with_agent.when_scoring_applied: After CV generation and before output

user_actions_based_on_score.score_90_plus: Approve and export PDF; congratulate candidate
user_actions_based_on_score.score_80_89: Offer minor refinement suggestions; enable export
user_actions_based_on_score.score_60_69: Require revision before export; detailed feedback
user_actions_based_on_score.score_below_60: Recommend professional review; major restructuring needed
